pi_items.source: Task description requirement 1: 'Add a new field called max_altitude_m to the Aircraft model'. Confirmed in verifier.py (line 86: Aircraft._meta.get_field('max_altitude_m')) and export_result.sh (line 29: grep 'max_altitude_m'). Field is used in multiple locations throughout the task specification.

Task description requirement 5: 'Set max_altitude_m = 120 for ALL existing Aircraft records'. Confirmed in verifier.py (line 91: qs.filter(max_altitude_m=120).count() and line 93: if count_120 == total_aircraft). Export script checks at line 98: result['count_with_120'] = qs.filter(max_altitude_m=120).count(). The verifier gives 25 points only when count_120 == total_aircraft, confirming all records must be set to this specific value.

Task description requirement 7: 'Verify the field appears in the admin panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/'. URL matches pattern from setup_aerobridge.sh (line 9: AEROBRIDGE_ADMIN_URL='http://localhost:8000/admin/') combined with requirement to verify field in Aircraft admin page. Consistent with Django admin path structure for the registry.Aircraft model.

privileged_info_summary: This task extends the Aerobridge Django model (openskies-sh/aerobridge project, archived Dec 2023) to add a new IntegerField called 'max_altitude_m' to the Aircraft model. The field must allow null values, be populated with a default value of 120 meters for all existing aircraft records, and be made visible in the Django admin panel at http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/. The task uses real fixture data from the official Aerobridge project containing sample aircraft, companies, and persons with GPS coordinates around India.
